divergence: Billionaires colonized the Moon and immediately complained about the neighborhood
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: Vem Shuttle Mycelium: The Towel That Kept on Giving, Now in Permanent Retirement
deck: Crisis deepens as Moonburg elite demand spore-credits for a luxury towel concierge service.
article:
In a broadcast ceremony on Halosca's networks, the Vem Shuttle Mycelium ended its run 45 years after its first towel at Gate 9. The shuttle, once known for its jingle and annual molt confusion, now sits as a relic. K't, its last operator, reportedly loops in perpetual distribution on billboards.

Billionaire X'yl appeared to lament the lack of a replacement concierge in Moonburg's bathhouses. "A towel is more than a towel; it’s a lifestyle statement," he said while tossing spore-credits. The Halosca Reef Council set aside 37 billion spore-credits for a new amenity lattice, even as the old network shuts down.

Tool-insect swarms keep building empty bays from habit after the molt erased clerk Shh and her spore-code knowledge. Moonburg elites grumble as the shuttle's absence grows noticeable.
author: K'yra Glimmer, Chief Nostalgia Correspondent

weather.condition: Overcast with a chance of complaints

oped.title: Mycelium Retirement Is Progress, Not Crisis—Pay the Spore-Credits
oped.author: Baron Vance Cassette, Certified Lunar Etiquette Consultant and Spore-Stock Holder Since '97
oped.body: The Moonburg elite are entirely correct to demand spore-credits for a luxury towel concierge. That Vem Shuttle Mycelium never knew its place; it kept giving without so much as a vaporwave playlist or holographic monogram. True refinement requires paid attendants who understand that billionaires did not colonize this crater to share fabric with anyone wearing last season’s chrome visors. My own modest holdings in spore futures will happily supply the new service, complete with mood-lit folding rituals unavailable to the masses. Blaming the elites misses the point entirely—the towel itself was the problem, fostering an unseemly expectation of free access. Retire it permanently, triple the credits, and perhaps the neighborhood will finally improve. One simply cannot put a price on exclusivity, though I have tried.

classifieds.text: Vintage Vem Shuttle Mycelium towels in permanent retirement, faintly glowing with spore residue, must liquidate before Moonburg elites demand concierge spore-credits; 3 floppies or best 56k offer.
Desperate Moonburg colony seeks towel mycelium wrangler fluent in spore-credits after the big retirement, hours spent soothing billionaire neighborhood gripes via AOL chat.
Lost: last working Vem Shuttle Mycelium unit that kept giving, now vanished into lunar dust; reward one crate of chrome palm decals if returned before spore-credit crisis hits.
Moon billionaire ISO new fungal towel service that won't retire, tired of complaining about the neighborhood, vaporwave aesthetics and 1999 dial-up only, no Earth spores please.

panels.caption: Moonburg penthouse, 1999 neon grid: 'Vem Shuttle Mycelium's done giving towels.'
Aide in chrome visor: 'Elites demand spore-credits for full concierge pickup.'
Billionaire: 'Fine. Evict the mold colony next door first.'

joke.setup: Billionaires dragged the Vem Shuttle Mycelium up to their lunar cul-de-sac so it could keep blotting out the view of Earth’s slums,
joke.punchline: but the towel finally retired after one too many complaints that the craters weren’t pastel enough.
joke.text: Billionaires dragged the Vem Shuttle Mycelium up to their lunar cul-de-sac so it could keep blotting out the view of Earth’s slums, but the towel finally retired after one too many complaints that the craters weren’t pastel enough.

sponsor_ads.headline: SporeWipe 3000: Mycelium's Chill Replacement
sponsor_ads.body: Billionaires on the dark side can finally mop up their neighborhood gripes without Vem Shuttle Mycelium's retired spores clogging the vents.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Neon grids sold separately. 1999 cassette not included.

sponsor_ads.headline: Lunar HOA Towel Memorial Sale
sponsor_ads.body: The permanent retirement forces every moon condo to swap their absorbent friend for our glitch-printed passive-aggressive doormats.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Complaints echo in vacuum. Aesthetic only.

sponsor_ads.headline: Vem's Final Shuttle Drop: Moon Rugs
sponsor_ads.body: Colonists tired of the block now get limited mycelium weaves that absorb both dust and endless neighborhood petitions.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Void where synthwaves fail to transmit.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Towel Evacuation Protocol
sponsor_ads.body: With the headline retirement, all Earth-to-Moon shipments pause until billionaires finish complaining about the new spore-free aesthetic.
sponsor_ads.tagline: 1999 vapor frequencies may cause static regrets.

stories.headline: Spore-Credit Vaults Register Record Withdrawals After Moonburg Towel Mandate
stories.deck: Mycelium traders report sudden liquidity crunch in the reef-arcology markets
stories.article:
Colonial fungal traders on Halosca watched spore-credits drain from vault hives yesterday as Moonburg residents filed simultaneous claims for the new Vem Shuttle Mycelium towel concierge. The bioluminescent reef-arcologies adjusted their tool-insect swarms to reroute nutrient flows toward luxury spore processing.

Analysts noted that one retired towel strain now requires fourteen separate credit ledgers per molting cycle, with traders K't and Shh confirming the imbalance will persist until next year's outer-layer shed.

real_world_echo: Nato allies announce £37bn for new missile project — mirrored as Halosca's Reef Council quietly reallocating spore-credits to a fresh amenity lattice while the old shuttle network receives a solemn send-off
